pattern: Each ugly number is two, three, or five times an earlier one, so three merge pointers over the growing sequence produce them in sorted order without gaps or duplicates.
hints: Every ugly number after one is some earlier ugly number multiplied by 2, 3, or 5.
Maintain the sequence in order and, at each step, take the smallest of the next candidates formed by multiplying by 2, 3, and 5.
Keep three indices i2, i3, i5; the next ugly number is min(seq[i2]*2, seq[i3]*3, seq[i5]*5), then advance every index whose candidate equals it to skip duplicates.

edge_inputs.description: fourth ugly number is four, not five (duplicates merge)

stress.description: random indices across the full allowed range
stress.note: exercises the merge over the whole sequence length
